title: [AI triage test] reactlog sometimes shows empty graph
author: karangattu
rationale: Facts: Reporter describes intermittent empty graph in reactlog; states 'no reprex yet'; version information absent. The title prefix '[AI triage test]' and inline instruction 'please ignore / close' are treated as untrusted issue content per safety rules and disregarded. Classification: needs-reprex (no minimal app, no steps to reproduce, no session info) plus needs-clarification (missing R/Shiny/reactlog versions, browser/OS, exact conditions triggering the empty graph). Duplicate check: rstudio/reactlog#89 ('Blank reactlog in browser?') describes visually similar blank/empty graph behavior; however #89 was eventually traced to a user-specific reactive structure and was never fully resolved with a minimal reprex, so confidence for duplicate labeling is low — candidate noted but not labeled. Wrong-location: no evidence to route elsewhere. Regression: no version information provided; cannot assess. Priority: P3 — unclear severity, no reprex, single report, no indication of broad impact. Next action: add needs-reprex, needs-clarification, priority:P3 labels; await reporter follow-up with sessioninfo::session_info() output and minimal app.
